metadata.title: Text field
metadata.description: The text field component captures single-line text input. Use it to collect short, free-form information like names, titles, or identifiers.

properties.description: An icon displayed inside the field to provide visual context about the expected input or field purpose. Commonly used for search fields, currency inputs, or to indicate field type. Accepts any icon name from the icon library or a custom string identifier.
The maximum number of characters allowed in the field.
The minimum number of characters required in the field.

A non-editable text value displayed immediately before the editable portion of the field. This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as https:// or +353. This text can't be edited by the user and is not included in the field's value. The prefix might not appear until the user interacts with the field. For example, an inline label might occupy the prefix position until the user focuses the field.

A non-editable text value displayed immediately after the editable portion of the field. This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as @shopify.com or %. This text can't be edited by the user and is not included in the field's value. The suffix might not appear until the user interacts with the field. For example, an inline label might occupy the suffix position until the user focuses the field.
The current text value in the field as a string. When setting this property programmatically, it updates the field's display value. When reading it, you get the user's current input.

properties.description: Controls browser autofill behavior for the field. Basic values: on - Enables autofill without specifying content type (default)off - Disables autofill for sensitive data or one-time codes Specific field values describe the expected data type. You can optionally prefix these with: section-${string} - Scopes autofill to a specific form section (when multiple forms exist on the same page)shipping or billing - Indicates whether the data is for shipping or billing purposesBoth section and group (for example, section-primary shipping email) Providing a specific autofill token helps browsers suggest more relevant saved data. Learn more about the set of autocomplete values supported in browsers.

The initial value of the field when it first loads. Unlike placeholder, this is a real value that the user can edit and that gets submitted with the form. Once the user starts typing, their input replaces it. Changing this property after the field has loaded has no effect. To update the field value at any time, use value instead.

Supplementary text displayed below the checkbox to provide additional context, instructions, or help. Use this to explain what checking the box means or provide guidance to users. This text is announced to screen readers.
An error message displayed below the checkbox to indicate validation problems. When set, the checkbox is styled with error indicators and the message is announced to screen readers.

The text displayed as the field label, which identifies the purpose of the field to users. This label is associated with the field for accessibility and helps users understand what information to provide.

Controls whether the label is visible to all users or only to screen readers. visible: The label is shown to everyone (default).exclusive: The label is visually hidden but still announced by screen readers. Use exclusive when the surrounding context makes the label redundant visually, but screen reader users still need it for clarity.
The placeholder text displayed in the field when it's empty, providing a hint about the expected input format or value.
Whether the field is read-only and can't be edited. Read-only fields remain focusable and their content is announced by screen readers.

Whether the field requires a value before form submission. Displays a visual indicator and adds semantic meaning, but doesn't automatically validate or show errors. Use the error property to display validation messages.
Whether the field is disabled, preventing any user interaction.
A unique identifier for the element. Use this to reference the element in JavaScript, link labels to form controls, or target specific elements for styling or scripting.
The name attribute for the field, used to identify the field's value when the form is submitted. Must be unique within the nearest containing form.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-text-field
  label="Store name"
  value="Jaded Pixel"
  placeholder="Become a merchant"
></s-text-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_field :store_name, label: "Store name", value: "Jaded Pixel", placeholder: "Become a merchant" %>

examples.name: Add a basic text field
examples.description: Add a single-line text input for collecting short-form information from merchants. This example shows a text field with a label, pre-filled value, and placeholder.
examples.html_code:
<s-text-field
  label="Store name"
  value="Jaded Pixel"
  placeholder="Become a merchant"
></s-text-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_field :store_name, label: "Store name", value: "Jaded Pixel", placeholder: "Become a merchant" %>

examples.name: Add an icon to a text field
examples.description: Add an icon to a text field to help merchants quickly identify its purpose. This example shows a text field with a search icon and placeholder text.

examples.name: Provide specific error messages for merchant context
examples.description: Provide specific error messages to tell merchants what went wrong and what correction is needed. This example shows three text fields contrasting a vague error, a specific validation error, and a business rule error.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <!-- Generic error (avoid) -->
  <s-text-field label="Product weight" error="Invalid value"></s-text-field>


  <!-- Specific error (preferred) -->
  <s-text-field
    label="Product weight"
    error="Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations"
  ></s-text-field>


  <!-- Business rule error -->
  <s-text-field
    label="SKU"
    error="SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products."
  ></s-text-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <!-- Generic error (avoid) -->
  <%= form.text_field :product_weight, label: "Product weight", error: "Invalid value" %>


  <!-- Specific error (preferred) -->
  <%= form.text_field :product_weight, label: "Product weight", error: "Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations" %>


  <!-- Business rule error -->
  <%= form.text_field :sku, label: "SKU", error: "SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products." %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Add a prefix and suffix
examples.description: Add a prefix or suffix to provide context for the expected value, such as a country code or card type. This example shows a phone number field with a prefix and a credit card field with a suffix.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <s-text-field label="Phone number" prefix="+03" />
  <s-text-field
    label="Credit Card Number"
    value="1234 5678 9012 3456"
    suffix="VISA"
  />
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="small">
  <%= form.text_field :phone_number, label: "Phone number", prefix: "+03" %>
  <%= form.text_field :credit_card_number, label: "Credit Card Number", value: "1234 5678 9012 3456", suffix: "VISA" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Add an accessory to a text field
examples.description: Place an interactive element like an icon or button inside a text field using the accessory slot. This example shows a text field with an info icon that triggers a tooltip.
examples.html_code:
<s-tooltip id="info-tooltip">This is info tooltip</s-tooltip>
<s-text-field label="Discount code" />
examples.erb_code:
<s-tooltip id="info-tooltip">This is info tooltip</s-tooltip>
<%= form.text_field :discount_code, label: "Discount code" %>

examples.name: Disable or make a text field read-only
examples.description: Prevent editing by making a text field read-only or fully disabled. This example shows a read-only store URL that merchants can copy and a disabled account ID.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-text-field
    label="Store URL"
    value="my-store.myshopify.com"
    readOnly
  ></s-text-field>


  <s-text-field
    label="Account ID"
    value="acct_12345"
    disabled
  ></s-text-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.text_field :store_url, label: "Store URL", value: "my-store.myshopify.com", readonly: true %>


  <%= form.text_field :account_id, label: "Account ID", value: "acct_12345", disabled: true %>
</s-stack>
